codeReview.summary: The originally reviewed commit only fixed shared-state prose and the schema-cleanup command, so it failed its own fix contract at the time. In the current repository state, the previously missing shared-schema implementation is now present in `pgtest.Open()`, and concrete async queue/drain helper usage exists in code, so the earlier scrutiny failure has been addressed overall with no remaining issue attributable to this feature alone.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `test-platform-worker` skill needs a stronger rule that worker-facing shared-state files must not claim canonical topology changes unless the underlying code path changed in the same feature or the worker explicitly scopes the note as pending follow-up.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The reviewed handoff for commit `8bfa66f` said `pgtest.Open()` was left unchanged, yet `.factory/library/environment.md` and `.factory/library/architecture.md` were updated to claim `pgtest.Open()` used `opentoggl_test`. This drift triggered the prior scrutiny failure even though the repository has since been corrected in `apps/backend/internal/testsupport/pgtest/pgtest.go`.

summary: Reviewed the originally failed fix against its handoff, diff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and current repository state. The commit `8bfa66f` by itself overstated shared-schema alignment, but the current repo now has `pgtest.Open()` on the canonical shared `opentoggl_test` schema plus concrete async helper usage, so the original blocking gap has been fixed and no new code issue remains for this feature line.
